changes_summary:
Revised all four phases based on flag feedback:

**FLAG-001 (Phase 2):** Replaced "keep calculateDuplicateFrame as preview-only" with an actual fix: stop passing `target_timeline_frame` from the travel-between-images duplicate path. Instead pass `timeline_frame` + `next_timeline_frame` so the SQL RPC computes placement. `calculateDuplicateFrame` stays for non-RPC callers but gets a doc comment clarifying SQL authority.

**FLAG-002 (Phase 3):** Replaced incorrect "split data into rows/meta only" approach. `resolveSelectedGenerationIdsForShotCreation` genuinely needs registry data (specifically `registry.assets[assetKey].generationId`). New approach: extract a stable `Record<string, string>` mapping of `assetKey → generationId` that only changes when actual generationId assignments change, not when effects register. This breaks the dependency on the full `data` object without losing required input.

**FLAG-003 (Phase 4):** Replaced the under-scoped "new RPC" approach. Since the upload pipeline already creates generations and shot_generation links during upload (each INSERT returns the full row), the fix is simply to surface this existing data to the caller. No new RPC needed — just accumulate and return what `processDroppedImages` already has, then remove the redundant post-upload fetch.

success_criteria.criterion: All existing tests pass after each phase
TimelineCanvas shot group menu no longer closes on mousedown inside the portal (Phase 1)
Travel-between-images duplicate path does NOT pass target_timeline_frame to the RPC — SQL computes frame placement (Phase 2)
No TypeScript code serves as the authoritative source for frame placement in any path that calls duplicate_as_new_generation (Phase 2)
ClipAction context menu does not flicker or lose items when EffectRegistry re-renders during an open menu (Phase 3)
shotActionsSnapshotRef workaround is fully removed from ClipAction.tsx (Phase 3)
selectionShotCreationState memo does not recompute when DynamicEffectRegistry._version changes but generationId mappings are unchanged (Phase 3)
createShotWithFilesPath makes zero post-upload queries to hydrate cache — uses data returned from upload pipeline (Phase 4)

calculateDuplicateFrame and ensureUniqueFrame remain available for non-RPC callers (addImageToShotHelpers, timelineDropHelpers, etc.) with doc comment clarifying SQL authority for duplicate_as_new_generation
Portal stopPropagation workaround has consistent comments across all three menus explaining the Node.contains() limitation
No new shared abstractions or utility files created — changes stay within existing files (useStableValue is acceptable as a small inline hook)
Manual smoke test: create shot from file upload, verify correct images appear in gallery without page refresh
Manual smoke test: duplicate image in travel-between-images tool, verify frame placement is correct

assumptions: The `addImageToShot` / `addImageToShotWithoutPosition` RPCs called during upload return the created shot_generation row (including timeline_frame). If they don't, the return type needs extending — but the generation metadata is definitely available from the generation INSERT.

The travel-between-images duplicate path can work without optimistic UI frame placement during the RPC call. If instant visual feedback is required, a tentative local position can be shown and corrected on RPC success, but the brief prioritizes SQL authority over optimistic preview.

A useStableValue hook (shallow-compare an object and return the previous reference if contents match) either exists in the codebase or is trivial to add (~10 lines). This is a common React pattern and not a new abstraction.

The three portal menus (ClipAction, SelectionContextMenu, TimelineCanvas) are the only portaled menus with custom click-outside handlers. Other menus use Radix/shadcn which handles portal containment internally.
